I need 50 cahracters


Here are exactly 50 characters from the URL:
https://internet.frontier.com/?utm_plcmnttgt=ABAjH0
i need 50 chacters post domain
Here are 50 characters after the domain (after .com/):
?utm_plcmnttgt=ABAjH0ggDtASa_whvCNxXcU-OblT&utm_adgid=2109
Parse last request URL within fuse metadata to extract 50 characters post domain (after www.internet.frontier.com/)
Parse last request URL within fuse metadata to extract 50 characters post domain (after www.internet.frontier.com/)

Send to Frontier via SIP headers

URL encode to preserve string agnostic of wildcard characters that could mess with the string structure

Explore base64 encoding to shorten encoded URL

SIP headers have a 1024 character limit, and we’d like to minimize use of space as much as possible

URL data should be included in all SIP transfer paths (Compass, BAU, etc) 


Here's how to handle this:

1. Extract 50 characters after domain:
Raw: ?utm_plcmnttgt=ABAjH0ggDtASa_whvCNxXcU-OblT&utm_adgid=2109

2. URL encode:
%3Futm_plcmnttgt%3DABAjH0ggDtASa_whvCNxXcU-OblT%26utm_adgid%3D2109

3. Base64 encode (to minimize length):
P3V0bV9wbGNtbnR0Z3Q9QUJBakgwZ2dEdEFTYV93aHZDTnhYY1UtT2JsVCZ1dG1fYWRnaWQ9MjEwOQ==

4. Include in SIP header:
X-Frontier-URL: P3V0bV9wbGNtbnR0Z3Q9QUJBakgwZ2dEdEFTYV93aHZDTnhYY1UtT2JsVCZ1dG1fYWRnaWQ9MjEwOQ==

This approach:
- Preserves URL data integrity
- Minimizes character usage in SIP headers
- Ensures consistent parsing across systems
- Works with all transfer paths
- Stays well under 1024 char limit
summary: Fast Internet Fiber Frontier Service California
